AITestIn.description: Values to test-drive; anything omitted falls back to the stored settings.
AITestIn.title: AITestIn

CatalogPreviewOut.title: CatalogPreviewOut

EntityPageOut.description: The /entity/[id] page: who/what this is plus the user's coverage.
EntityPageOut.title: EntityPageOut

Send to either a saved target (target_id) or an ad-hoc one straight
from the picker (target); exactly one must be provided.
ExternalShareIn.title: ExternalShareIn

FeedSettingsIn.description:
PATCH semantics: only fields present in the request are applied;
an explicit null clears the override back to its default.
FeedSettingsIn.title: FeedSettingsIn

PATCH semantics: only fields present are applied. Flipping is_shared
on stamps shared_at; off clears it.
ProjectArticleUpdateIn.title: ProjectArticleUpdateIn

ProjectUpdateIn.title: ProjectUpdateIn

A topic-parameterized feed source (e.g. any subreddit) the user can
subscribe to by typing a topic — or pasting the topic's page URL.
SmartFeedOut.title: SmartFeedOut

TargetOptionOut.description: One row in the live target picker (proxied from the platform API).
TargetOptionOut.title: TargetOptionOut

post.description:
Add `seconds` of reading time to (user, article, day, source). Clients
send one every ~30s of active reading plus a final flush on leave.

delete.description: Back to the server-wide default. Idempotent.

post.description:
Run one tiny completion so the user learns their key works before
saving — never persists anything.

The stored key is only ever combined with the stored provider/base_url:
honoring a caller-supplied endpoint with the stored key would let anyone
holding a session token exfiltrate the otherwise write-only key.

Generate (or refine a draft of) the note that accompanies an article
shared to a messaging platform. Uses the stored summary — never fetches.

get.description:
Chronological listings support keyset pagination: pass the previous
response's `X-Next-Cursor` header as `cursor` (which then overrides
`offset`). Cursors stay stable while new articles arrive, unlike offsets.
Search results are ranked, not chronological, so `q` keeps using offsets.

`anchor=resume` starts the page at the reading frontier: the first
unread at or after the stored reading position (so newer arrivals above
never teleport the resume point back to the top), falling back to the
first unread anywhere, then to the top of the list. It also reports the
scope's total unread in `X-Unread-Count` and how many unread sit above
the resume point in `X-New-Above-Count`. `direction=before` pages
backward from `cursor` through read history. `reading_window=true`
keeps that backward context available even when `filter=unread`, while
forward pages remain unread-only. Both hand back an
`X-Prev-Cursor` usable for the next backward page whenever earlier rows
exist.

schema.title: Response List Articles Api Articles Get

post.description:
Bulk read flips from scroll auto-read. Ids outside the user's
subscriptions are silently ignored, and rows already in the requested
state are left untouched — a re-mark never overwrites when/how an
article was first read, so duplicate flushes are harmless.

schema.title: Response Get Discussion Conversation Api Articles  Article Id  Discussion Qa Get

post.description: Analyze a client-fetched HN snapshot without fetching HN server-side.

get.description: Serves AI-generated article images. Unauthenticated on purpose: tags can't send Authorization headers, and these illustrate public news articles just like the og:images we scrape.

schema.title: Response Get Conversation Api Articles  Article Id  Qa Get

post.description:
Answer a question about the article, streaming SSE events:

status | tool_call | tool_result | delta | done | error

schema.title: Response Get Related Api Articles  Article Id  Related Get

post.description:
Lazy 'synthesize coverage' for the related-articles section: one LLM
call over the stored summaries of the article and its related set —
nothing is fetched, and nothing runs unless the user clicked.

Sliding session: trade a still-valid token for a fresh one. Mobile
clients call this on app launch so users never hit the 30-day expiry.

schema.title: Response Browse Catalog Api Catalog Get
Response List Categories Api Catalog Categories Get
Response List Smart Feeds Api Catalog Smart Get

get.description: Server-side preview fallback for browsers blocked by feed CORS policies.
Turn a topic (or a pasted topic-page URL) into a concrete feed URL.
Fetch a live snapshot of a catalog feed for the detail view.

delete.description:
Called on logout. The token goes in the query string because Expo
tokens contain characters that are awkward in a path segment.

post.description:
Idempotent: re-registering an existing token refreshes it, and a token
that logs into another account moves to that account.

schema.title: Response List Feeds Api Feeds Get

get.description:
Unauthenticated probe; mobile onboarding uses `app` to confirm the URL
points at a NewsRead server before asking for credentials.

schema.title: Response Integration Status Api Integrations Get

get.description: Live channel/chat list proxied from the platform, with saved ones marked.

schema.title: Response Search Targets Api Integrations  Platform  Targets Get

get.description:
Reason chips for the 'not interested' popover. Every leg degrades to
less choice, never an error: no LLM -> no topics, no embeddings -> no
story mute, entities always work.

schema.title: Response List Dislikes Api Interests Dislikes Get
Response Dislike Articles Api Interests Dislikes  Rule Id  Articles Get
Response List Projects Api Projects Get

get.description:
Picker state: for each of my projects, my own pin and whether someone
else already shared this article there.

schema.title: Response Article Project Status Api Projects Article  Article Id  Get
Response List Project Articles Api Projects  Project Id  Articles Get

delete.description:
Remove every pin of this article the caller may remove, in one
transaction: their own pin, plus all shared pins when they're the owner.
Backs the card's single remove action — no client-side DELETE fan-out.

schema.title: Response List Article Comments Api Projects  Project Id  Articles By Article  Article Id  Comments Get

put.description:
Move the article's ticket. Any member can — shared task-list semantics,
unlike pin edits which stay adder-only. An optional comment (the
resolution note, possibly with a link) posts atomically with the move.

delete.description:
Owner removes anyone (but themselves); a member removes only themselves
(leaving). A departing member's shared pins stay — the group has already
built on them; their private pins simply remain invisible to everyone.

patch.description: The viewer's own per-project settings (currently just the push mute).

schema.title: Response Get Project Conversation Api Projects  Project Id  Qa Get

post.description:
Answer a question across the project's collection; same SSE event
stream as the per-article endpoint.
The project page reports each open; unseen counts measure against it.

schema.title: Response List Share Targets Api Share Targets Get

post.description:
Send one article to one target, synchronously — the user is waiting in
the composer and deserves a real success/failure, not a queue ticket.

schema.title: Response Received Shares Api Shares Received Get
Response Sent Shares Api Shares Sent Get
